data.content:
### Install BotID package with package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/botid/get-started

Install the BotID package using different package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun). This is the first step to add bot protection to your Vercel project.

```bash
pnpm i botid
```

```bash
yarn i botid
```

```bash
npm i botid
```

```bash
bun i botid
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Package Manager with vercel.json installCommand

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/package-managers

This snippet demonstrates how to configure a specific package manager, such as pnpm, for a Vercel deployment using the `installCommand` property within the `vercel.json` file. This setting overrides the default package manager used by Vercel for that particular deployment.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "installCommand": "pnpm install"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install AI SDK and OpenAI packages with package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/functions/streaming-functions

Install the required `ai` and `@ai-sdk/openai` packages using various package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). These packages enable streaming text generation from OpenAI models on Vercel Functions.

```bash
pnpm i ai openai
```

```bash
yarn i ai openai
```

```bash
npm i ai openai
```

```bash
bun i ai openai
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI using Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/analytics/quickstart

Install the Vercel CLI tool as a prerequisite for Vercel project management. Supports multiple package managers including npm, yarn, pnpm, and bun. Choose the command appropriate for your project's package manager.

```bash
npm i vercel
```

```bash
yarn i vercel
```

```bash
pnpm i vercel
```

```bash
bun i vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Install Nitro Package Manager

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/full-stack/tanstack-start

Install the Nitro package across multiple package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). Choose the command matching your project's package manager to add Nitro to your TanStack Start application.

```pnpm
pnpm i nitro
```

```yarn
yarn i nitro
```

```npm
npm i nitro
```

```bun
bun i nitro
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel SDK with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/sdk

Install the @vercel/sdk package using pnpm, npm, or yarn. Choose the package manager that matches your project setup.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/sdk
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/sdk
```

```bash
yarn add @vercel/sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies with Multiple Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/private-registry

Installation commands for setting up dependencies using four different package managers: npm, yarn, pnpm, and bun. Each command installs the project's dependencies as specified in the package.json file.

```bash
npm i
```

```bash
yarn i
```

```bash
pnpm i
```

```bash
bun i
```

--------------------------------

### Pin Package Manager Version with Corepack in package.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/builds/configure-a-build

This configuration snippet for `package.json` demonstrates how to use Corepack to pin a specific version of a package manager, such as pnpm. This experimental Node.js feature allows projects to ensure consistent package manager usage across environments. Enable Corepack by setting the `ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_COREPACK` environment variable to `1`.

```json
{
  "packageManager": "pnpm@7.5.1"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Flags SDK with package manager

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/feature-flags/flags-explorer/getting-started

Install the flags package using your preferred package manager (pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun). This package provides convenience methods, components, and types for communicating with Flags Explorer.

```bash
pnpm i flags
```

```bash
yarn i flags
```

```bash
npm i flags
```

```bash
bun i flags
```

--------------------------------

### Install AI SDK Dependencies with Package Manager

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Installation command for adding the Vercel AI SDK and OpenAI integration packages to your project. Supports multiple package managers including pnpm, yarn, npm, and bun.

```Shell
pnpm i ai openai
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/og Package with Multiple Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/og-image-generation

Installation commands for @vercel/og package across different package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). Required for Node.js projects; not needed for Next.js App Router as it's pre-included. Execute one command based on your project's package manager.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/og
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/og
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/og
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/og
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/speed-insights Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/speed-insights/quickstart

Add the @vercel/speed-insights package to your project using your preferred package manager. This package is required for framework-based implementations.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/speed-insights
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/speed-insights
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/speed-insights
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/speed-insights
```

--------------------------------

### Create Next.js Project with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/edge-config/integrations/devcycle-edge-config

Create a new Next.js project using multiple package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). Required for setting up a Vercel project with DevCycle integration.

```bash
pnpm create next-app@latest
```

```bash
yarn create next-app@latest
```

```bash
npx create-next-app@latest
```

```bash
bunx create-next-app@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI with package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/cli

Install Vercel CLI using multiple package managers including npm, yarn, pnpm, and bun. Each command downloads and installs the latest version of the Vercel CLI tool for command-line access to Vercel platform features.

```bash
pnpm i vercel
```

```bash
yarn i vercel
```

```bash
npm i vercel
```

```bash
bun i vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Corepack and Install pnpm Dependencies

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/ecommerce/bigcommerce

This command enables Corepack, a tool to manage Node.js package manager versions, then activates pnpm and uses it to install all project dependencies. It ensures the correct package manager is used for the Catalyst project setup.

```bash
corepack enable pnpm && pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel Toolbar package using package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/vercel-toolbar/in-production-and-localhost/add-to-localhost

Installs the `@vercel/toolbar` package into your project using common Node.js package managers. This package is a prerequisite for integrating the Vercel Toolbar into your local development environment.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/toolbar
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/toolbar
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/toolbar
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/toolbar
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/edge-config/integrations/devcycle-edge-config

Install or update the latest version of Vercel CLI using multiple package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). This is a prerequisite for setting up the DevCycle Edge Config integration.

```bash
pnpm i -g vercel@latest
```

```bash
yarn global add vercel@latest
```

```bash
npm i -g vercel@latest
```

```bash
bun add -g vercel@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel Blob Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/storage/vercel-blob/server-upload

Installs the `@vercel/blob` package using various package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). This is the initial step to enable Vercel Blob functionality within your project.

```Terminal
pnpm i @vercel/blob
```

```Terminal
yarn add @vercel/blob
```

```Terminal
npm i @vercel/blob
```

```Terminal
bun add @vercel/blob
```

--------------------------------

### Install `@vercel/related-projects` NPM Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos

Install the `@vercel/related-projects` package using your preferred package manager (npm, bun, yarn, or pnpm). This package provides utility functions for easily accessing and parsing information about related projects within your Vercel deployments.

```bash
npm i @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
bun add @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
yarn add @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
pnpm add @vercel/related-projects
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/remix package with package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/full-stack/remix

Install the @vercel/remix package using pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun. This package exposes useful types and utilities for Remix apps deployed on Vercel, such as json, defer, and createCookie functions.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/remix
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/remix
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/remix
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/remix
```

--------------------------------

### Install AI SDK with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/ai/openai

Install the Vercel AI SDK in your project using your preferred package manager (pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun). The AI SDK enables seamless integration of OpenAI models into Vercel projects and supports multiple frameworks.

```shell
pnpm i ai
```

```shell
yarn i ai
```

```shell
npm i ai
```

```shell
bun i ai
```

--------------------------------

### Install Workflow Development Kit (WDK) Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/workflow

This section provides commands to install the Workflow Development Kit (WDK) package using various package managers like pnpm, yarn, npm, and bun. WDK is a TypeScript framework for building scalable and durable applications on Vercel.

```bash
pnpm i workflow
```

```bash
yarn i workflow
```

```bash
npm i workflow
```

```bash
bun i workflow
```

--------------------------------

### Install DevCycle Edge Config Packages with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/edge-config/integrations/devcycle-edge-config

Install both the DevCycle Vercel Edge Config integration package and the Edge Config SDK using multiple package managers. Required for the DevCycle feature flag management integration.

```bash
pnpm i @devcycle/vercel-edge-config @vercel/edge-config
```

```bash
yarn add @devcycle/vercel-edge-config @vercel/edge-config
```

```bash
npm i @devcycle/vercel-edge-config @vercel/edge-config
```

```bash
bun add @devcycle/vercel-edge-config @vercel/edge-config
```

--------------------------------

### Define vercel-build script in package.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Configure a custom build script in package.json that runs before Vercel Function deployment. The nearest package.json to the function is used for both installing dependencies and building. This allows preprocessing or generating files needed by the function.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "vercel-build": "node ./build.js"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Python Dependencies with `requirements.txt` for Vercel Functions

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

This `requirements.txt` file lists specific Python package dependencies, in this case, FastAPI at version 0.117.1. Vercel automatically reads this file and installs all listed packages during the build process, ensuring that all necessary libraries are available for your Python functions.

```text
fastapi==0.117.1
```

--------------------------------

### Start Next.js JSS Application Locally

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/sitecore

Execute commands to install project dependencies, build the application, and start it in development mode. This prepares and launches your Next.js JSS application for local testing and development using various package managers.

```pnpm
pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm dev
```

```yarn
yarn && yarn build && yarn dev
```

```npm
npm install && npm run build && npm run dev
```

```bun
bun install && bun run build && bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Configure build script in package.json for Vercel

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/errors/error-list

Define a build script in package.json that performs a static build and outputs to a public directory. This configuration is required for Vercel deployments when no api directory or vercel.json file exists. The build script should specify the framework build command with the output directory set to 'public' at the project root.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "[my-framework] build --output public"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start Next.js Development Server with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/contentful

Install dependencies and start the Next.js development server with pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun. The application will run on http://localhost:3000 and support hot module reloading for development.

```pnpm
pnpm install && pnpm run dev
```

```yarn
yarn && yarn dev
```

```npm
npm install && npm run dev
```

```bun
bun install && bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install SvelteKit Vercel adapter with package manager

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/full-stack/sveltekit

Install the @sveltejs/adapter-vercel package using various package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun). This adapter enables automatic detection of Vercel deployment and provides version stability.

```pnpm
pnpm i @sveltejs/adapter-vercel
```

```yarn
yarn i @sveltejs/adapter-vercel
```

```npm
npm i @sveltejs/adapter-vercel
```

```bun
bun i @sveltejs/adapter-vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Enable On-Demand Concurrent Builds with Vercel SDK

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/managing-builds

Enable on-demand concurrent builds using the official Vercel SDK for JavaScript/TypeScript. This method provides type-safe access to the Vercel API and requires authentication via a Bearer token. Supports specifying project ID, team ID, and resource configuration including build machine type.

```JavaScript
import { Vercel } from '@vercel/sdk';

const vercel = new Vercel({
  bearerToken: '<YOUR_BEARER_TOKEN_HERE>',
});

async function run() {
  const result = await vercel.projects.updateProject({
    idOrName: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
    teamId: 'YOUR_TEAM_ID',
    requestBody: {
      resourceConfig: {
        elasticConcurrencyEnabled: true,
        buildMachineType: 'enhanced',
      },
    },
  });

  console.log(result);
}

run();
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies with Multiple Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway/framework-integrations/langfuse

Install required packages including LangFuse, OpenAI client, dotenv for environment variables, and TypeScript type definitions. Supports pnpm, yarn, npm, and bun package managers.

```bash
pnpm i langfuse openai dotenv @types/node
```

```bash
yarn i langfuse openai dotenv @types/node
```

```bash
npm i langfuse openai dotenv @types/node
```

```bash
bun i langfuse openai dotenv @types/node
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/cms/agility-cms

Install the Vercel CLI package manager using pnpm. This tool is required to pull environment variables from Vercel to your local project during development and deployment setup.

```bash
pnpm i vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/remix package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Install the `@vercel/remix` package using pnpm to access Vercel-specific utilities for Remix applications, such as enhanced `json`, `defer`, and `createCookie` functions. This package replaces standard Remix utility imports for better Vercel feature integration.

```shell
pnpm i @vercel/remix
```

--------------------------------

### Install AI SDK and Dependencies Across Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway/getting-started

Install the AI SDK package and required dependencies (dotenv, @types/node, tsx, typescript) using various package managers. Dotenv manages environment variables, tsx runs TypeScript code, typescript provides the compiler, and @types/node provides Node.js type definitions.

```bash
pnpm i ai dotenv @types/node tsx typescript
```

```bash
yarn i ai dotenv @types/node tsx typescript
```

```bash
npm i ai dotenv @types/node tsx typescript
```

```bash
bun i ai dotenv @types/node tsx typescript
```

--------------------------------

### Install Edge Config SDK with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/edge-config/integrations/devcycle-edge-config

Install the Vercel Edge Config SDK using multiple package managers. This is required to interact with Edge Config in your application.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/edge-config
```

```bash
yarn add @vercel/edge-config
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/edge-config
```

```bash
bun add @vercel/edge-config
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/analytics Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/analytics/quickstart

Add the @vercel/analytics package to your project using your preferred package manager. This package provides analytics tracking capabilities for multiple frameworks.

```bash
npm i @vercel/analytics
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/analytics
```

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/analytics
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/analytics
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenTelemetry and Vercel OTEL packages

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/tracing/instrumentation

Installs the necessary OpenTelemetry API and Vercel OpenTelemetry packages using different Node.js package managers. These packages are required to enable instrumentation in your project.

```bash
pnpm i @opentelemetry/api @vercel/otel
```

```bash
yarn i @opentelemetry/api @vercel/otel
```

```bash
npm i @opentelemetry/api @vercel/otel
```

```bash
bun i @opentelemetry/api @vercel/otel
```
